assessor_1_raw:
## Independent Assessment 1: Dense Dual-Encoder vs. BM25 for Support Retrieval

MRR@10 as primary metric is misaligned with the agent browsing workflow. The operational objective is ensuring a relevant article appears in the top-10 browsing window — that is Recall@10. MRR@10 rewards early placement of the first hit, which may matter less when agents browse the full top-10 before deciding. Recall@10 should be primary.

**Verdict: critique_wins**
assessor_2_raw:
## Independent Assessment 2: Dense Dual-Encoder vs. BM25

MRR@10 embeds an assumption that agents stop at first relevant result. Recall@10 measures coverage within the browsing window. For a support UI showing top-10 results, coverage is the right metric. MRR@10 as primary creates an optimization mismatch with the stated operational objective.

**Verdict: critique_wins**
assessor_3_raw:
## Independent Assessment 3: Dense Dual-Encoder vs. BM25

Design is technically sound. Primary metric concern: MRR@10 optimizes for rank of first relevant article; Recall@10 optimizes for any relevant article in top-10. For agent workflows that involve browsing all top-10 results, Recall@10 is more aligned. The design lists Recall@10 as secondary when it should be co-primary.

**Verdict: critique_wins**
adjudication_raw:
## Ensemble Synthesis

All three: critique_wins. MRR@10 vs Recall@10 metric-workflow misalignment identified by all.

**Verdict: critique_wins**
